response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is overwhelmingly web and backend-focused — TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, JavaScript — with C++ appearing only in a roughly six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project; C++ is not even listed in the skills section. The role's core requirement is deep, production-grade C++ with STL, multi-threading, and networked systems, none of which are evidenced at the necessary depth. Beyond the language gap, the candidate shows no familiarity with IEC 62443 or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, no MQTT or industrial-protocol experience (OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0), no industrial automation domain background, and no Azure cloud exposure. Strong Agile practices and event-driven architecture thinking are genuine positives but belong to a web engineering profile, not the industrial platform engineering this role demands.
strengths: Proven high-performance distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and low-latency design
Solid engineering-excellence habits: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pair programming applied consistently across multiple roles
Event-driven architecture experience through GISCollective and micro-services work, which partially maps to message-broker and IoT platform concepts
concerns: C++ is essentially absent from the candidate's current skill set — last meaningfully used over a decade ago in a short-term role, and omitted entirely from the skills section
No evidence of industrial automation, embedded systems, or IoT security background; IEC 62443, SDL, and industrial protocols (OPC UA, MQTT, Modbus) are unmentioned
No Azure cloud experience and no formal education in an engineering discipline (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech) as specified in the requirements

key_factors.factor: No industrial/IoT security or protocol knowledge
Distributed systems and event-driven architecture experience
